phase.name: Grill-me + auto-mode embeddings across ev-loop / archive / ev-run

contract.goal: Rewrite Step 2.1 (Negotiate) of skills/ev-loop-interactive/SKILL.md to add the grill-me + auto-mode pattern from PLAN.md Phase 6. Original 4-line step replaced with 3 paths: (a) default clean-draft single approve/redirect; (b) grill-me on ambiguous fields walking ONLY hedge-worded/empty fields as separate questions; (c) auto-mode running evaluator-contract-fit against the contract with silent-OR-budget(3 × 5). Auto-mode emits the 3 D1 events (auto-mode-entered, auto-mode-converged, auto-mode-budget-exhausted) with surface='ev-loop-interactive'.
acceptance_criteria: Step 2.1 covers 3 paths with 3 concrete ambiguity types named (empty inputs[]; hedge-worded ACs with literal phrase examples; undefined disqualifiers when unit touches >2 files).
Auto-mode block has 3 explicit emit-lines for the D1 events with surface='ev-loop-interactive'.
Substrate 3 × 5 default (per docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md table for this surface) cited inline.
Human-paired mode emits no auto-mode events (conversation is the audit trail).
'Human is in the loop' assertion preserved with auto-mode as the explicit opt-out.
npm test green at 485 (markdown-only change to skill body).
rules_applied: PLAN.md Phase 6 lines 425-431 dictates the surface, the budget, and the no-auto-commit-on-exhaust rule.
AGENT-CONVENTIONS § Auto-mode and the two-budget shape provides the 3 × 5 default.
inputs: skills/ev-loop-interactive/SKILL.md pre-change at line 143 (the 4-line Negotiate step)
cli/lib/types.ts D1 of this phase (the 3 auto-mode event types)

actions: Expanded Step 2.1 from 4 lines to ~55 lines. Three sub-paths under a single step header (Default; Grill-me on ambiguous fields; Auto-mode).
Default path: single approve/redirect on the whole contract (unchanged human-paired behavior when contract has no ambiguity).

Grill-me-on-ambiguous-fields path: walk ONLY the ambiguous fields as separate one-at-a-time questions before showing the full contract for final approve/redirect. Three concrete ambiguity types: (a) empty inputs[]; (b) hedge-worded ACs with literal example phrases ('should probably', 'might want to', 'ideally', 'consider whether', or any AC that doesn't tell a reviewer how to falsify it); (c) undefined disqualifiers when unit touches >2 files.

Auto-mode path: evaluator-contract-fit audits contract against inputs; silent-panel (approved with zero findings) = convergence; flagged finding = redirect → ambiguity walk round. Substrate 3 × 5 default cited (per AGENT-CONVENTIONS table). Budget exhaust surfaces unresolved ambiguities to operator with a structured error and stops — does NOT auto-commit half-ambiguous contract.

Added auto-mode event emissions block: auto-mode-entered on entry, auto-mode-converged on silent-panel, auto-mode-budget-exhausted on cap. All carry surface: 'ev-loop-interactive' and the documented detail shape from D1.
Preserved 'human is in the loop' closing paragraph: auto-mode is the explicit operator opt-out.
Derived evaluator panel (contract-fit). Invoked /guild-validate. Verdict: approved on round 1. All AC items verified by spot-check.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on this branch. D1 declared the 3 auto-mode event types. D2 rewrites the ev-loop's unit-contract negotiation step to use them. D3 will add the same auto-mode pattern to /loom-archive; D4 will add it to /ev-run.
notes_for_pr: Grill-me-on-ambiguous-fields is the substantive shape change. The default path is unchanged from prior behavior — clean drafts still go through single approve/redirect. The grill-me path only activates when the contract field-shape says ambiguity exists; concrete definition prevents the loop from over-interviewing on every contract.

Hedge-worded ACs detection uses literal phrase examples in the skill body ('should probably', 'might want to', 'ideally', 'consider whether'). The list isn't a regex match — the skill body's intent is to train the model's reading of contracts to recognize hedge-shaped sentences as falsifiability gaps.

Auto-mode does NOT auto-commit a half-ambiguous contract on budget exhaust. The unit fails to negotiate cleanly and the loop surfaces unresolved ambiguities to the operator. This matches the substrate's broader posture: auto-mode is the operator opt-out for routine decisions, not a bypass for blocking gates.
The skill body is now 564 lines (was 505 before D2). Step 2.1 is the largest single step at ~55 lines, which feels right given it documents three substantively different paths.
